metric_value: Significant upside potential for synergy in many areas; primary focus must be clarity of message and execution excellence
notes: Peter Kastner's core thesis as Aberdeen CRO

metric_value: Combination could create industry powerhouse IF execution excellence surpasses pre-merger efforts
notes: Conditional prediction; hinged on execution quality

metric_value: Partial success: merger cost savings exceeded targets by 30% in H2 2002 ($651M vs plan), but Fiorina ousted Feb 2005; HP split into HP Inc. and HPE in 2015
notes: Execution challenges materialized; HP never became undisputed industry powerhouse

metric_value: Success must stem from the PC business — Compaq's Wintel strategy should be adopted by new HP
notes: Aberdeen's Windows Perspective InSight

metric_value: PC sales dropped 19% to $4.8B in first combined quarter (Q3 FY2002); Personal Systems cut operating loss by 50%+ sequentially in Q4 FY2002
notes: Initial struggles then recovery in PC division

metric_value: New HP must create order in high-end Unix platform strategy — HP-UX and Tru64 require rationalization
notes: Aberdeen's Unix Perspective InSight

metric_value: HP discontinued Tru64 advanced features Dec 2004; migrated to HP-UX on Itanium; many Tru64 developers laid off
notes: Aberdeen's concern proved valid; rationalization was painful for customers

metric_name: Linux business enhancement from Compaq acquisition
metric_value: HP acquisition of Compaq enhances Linux business for the new company
notes: Aberdeen's Linux Perspective InSight

metric_value: HP reported 18th consecutive quarter of Linux market-share leadership in Q4 FY2002
notes: Linux prediction validated; HP maintained Linux leadership

metric_value: HP/Compaq combination presents prescription for storage success — combined storage portfolio is complementary
notes: Aberdeen's Storage Perspective InSight

metric_value: 17% sequential increase in on-line storage revenues; strong StorageWorks wins including $200M with Ericsson
notes: Storage synergy prediction partially validated

metric_value: HP/Compaq combined IT services must offer more than size; service mix quality critical
notes: Aberdeen's Services Perspective InSight

metric_value: Technology services revenue fell 7% to $3B in first combined quarter — services underperformed
notes: Services struggled initially; later strong: $1.5B CIBC win, $225M VA contract

metric_value: HP/Compaq must focus on customer outreach, clarity of messages, and marketing execution excellence; programs should start immediately
notes: Core recommendation from Kastner/Dorin Corporate Overview piece

metric_value: We've kept our eye on the ball. We did well.
notes: Fiorina quote from Q3 FY2002 results call, 6 weeks post-merger close

metric_name: HP Q3 FY2002 revenue (first combined quarter)
metric_value: $16.5 billion (vs. $18.6B combined year-earlier)
notes: First quarter as combined entity; revenue decline reflects tech sector downturn

metric_value: $18.0 billion (vs $16.5B prior quarter — 9% sequential increase)
notes: Recovery trajectory visible by Q4

metric_value: $651 million in H2 2002 — 30% above plan; 12,500 net workforce reductions, 25% above plan
notes: Largest tech merger cost savings program; exceeded targets

metric_value: 58% sequential revenue increase in Q4 FY2002; HP ranked #1 on TOP500 Supercomputer Sites enterprise category
notes: Strong high-end server performance

metric_value: 30% worldwide server market share per Gartner Dataquest (leading all vendors)
notes: HP/Compaq combined achieved dominant server market position

metric_value: Printing systems revenue increased 10% to $4.7 billion — most profitable division
notes: Printing remained HP's anchor profitable business

metric_value: Big computer systems revenue fell 22% to $3.8 billion; $422M operating loss called 'unacceptable' by Capellas
notes: Overlapping product lines between HP and Compaq caused enterprise revenue pain

metric_value: 30 largest new contracts averaged $120M+ each; totaling $3.7B in new long-term revenue
notes: Strong enterprise sales momentum by Q4

metric_value: Numerous naysayers exist but Aberdeen sees significant upside potential that should not be ignored
notes: Merger narrowly survived shareholder vote in March 2002; Hewlett/Packard family opposed

metric_value: Capellas predicted PC division would return to profitability in H1 FY2003
notes: Capellas left HP November 2002 to become CEO of WorldCom/MCI

metric_value: HP planned to migrate Unix customer base from RISC (Alpha/PA-RISC) to HP-UX on Itanium
notes: Itanium strategy was HP/Intel joint initiative; ultimately failed by 2021

metric_value: Intel discontinued Itanium in 2021; HP's Itanium-based Unix strategy failed to achieve dominance
notes: x86/Linux ultimately displaced Itanium as enterprise platform

metric_value: Dell targeting more computer resellers instead of just selling directly — channel expansion
notes: Dell's channel push was competitive response to HP/Compaq combined channel strength

metric_value: 15,000 net workforce reductions planned; 4,740 eliminated in first quarter post-merger
notes: Restructuring as part of merger integration

metric_value: HP split into HP Inc. (consumer/printing) and Hewlett Packard Enterprise (servers/services) in November 2015
notes: Ultimate outcome: merger did not create enduring unified powerhouse; company split 13 years later
